breaking.title: West Asia War LIVE: U.S. strikes kill at least seven at Iranian Army base
breaking.summary: Over 260 people wounded in latest U.S. airstrikes on Iran; Tehran attacks Jordan as U.S. reimposes its blockade on Iran

breaking.title: Middle East war: Trump weighs 'devastating strikes'; Iran targets US bases in Middle East - key points
breaking.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guard has asserted that the Strait of Hormuz will stay closed until the United States changes its course of action. This comes on the heels of the US conducting new strikes near the Strait and reinstating a naval blockade. Iran has also announced attacks on US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain, exacerbating regional tensions after a recently collapsed truce.

breaking.title: U.S. strikes Iran for third day, will reimpose blockade
breaking.summary: President Trump said a deal with the Islamic Republic was still possible even as attacks were carried out, with Iran striking two ships in the strategic waterway and killing one crew member

breaking.title: Trump threatens to bomb bridges and power plants unless Iran resumes talks
breaking.summary: Trump's comments aired as the two countries exchanged fire for the fourth day in a row and the US resumed blockading Iranian ports.

breaking.title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Jumps 350 Points As US Drops 20% Fee On Ships Crossing Hormuz
breaking.summary: Sensex Today, Stock Market, Nifty, Share Market Live News Updates

breaking.title: What are the gains from the India-U.K. trade deal? | Explained
breaking.summary: Zero tariffs. Cheaper whisky and British cars. Major benefits for Indian workers in the U.K. The India-U.K. trade deal could reshape business on both sides, and here’s the complete picture

breaking.title: FBI adds gangster Nitish Kaushal to its most wanted list
breaking.summary: Kaushal, an Indian national, allegedly carried out acts of violence on behalf of the Bhagwanpuria organised crime group, including, but not limited to, kidnappings and assaults, the FBI said

breaking.title: Why China’s 4.3% growth is worrying its government | Explainer
breaking.summary: It's the slowest growth since 2022, when China was still in the grips of the pandemic, and lower than the government's already-lowered annual target of 4.5-5%; the country is facing multiple challenges of a real estate slowdown, sluggish consumption, and serious challenges in the job market
